zh.of Danbury, Essex, Engl: 英國英格蘭艾可丹波瑞
zh.Danbury, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可丹波瑞
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III於1478年出生在英國艾可丹波瑞時，他的父親Thomas Darcy19歲，母親Lady Margaret Harleston23歲。他於大約1504年與Elizabeth Wentworth在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1508年9月30日在英國艾可馬爾頓享年30歲。

zh-hans.of Danbury, Essex, Engl: 英国英格兰艾可丹波瑞
zh-hans.Danbury, Essex, England: 英国英格兰艾可丹波瑞
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III于1478年出生在英国艾可丹波瑞时，他的父亲Thomas Darcy19岁，母亲Lady Margaret Harleston23岁。他于大约1504年与Elizabeth Wentworth在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1508年9月30日在英国艾可馬爾頓享年30岁。

nameMeanings.name: Darcy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Eamon, Brendan, Eamonn, Maeve, Malachy, Niamh, Seamus.  French Gisele, Julien, Normand, Alain, Armand, Cecile, Clemence, Dominique, Emile, Gilles, Jean-Paul, Laure.
English (Lancashire) and Irish: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Arcy in Manche, France, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’.

Irish: in Ireland, this is generally the Norman name (see 1 above), established in Meath by Sir William D'Arcy in the 1360s, but it was also adopted for Ó Dorchaidhe ‘descendant of the dark one’, from dorcha ‘dark, gloomy’, a Connacht name that fell together with the Norman surname, which was introduced by Sir William D'Arcy and Sir John D'Arcy, Chief Justiciar of Ireland in the 14th century.

variants.name: Darsey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Darcy   2.
Scottish: from Dairsie in Fife.

variants.name: Darcey
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Darcy  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Little
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a small man (if not ironic), or distinguishing epithet for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, from Middle English littel, Old English lȳtel (see Light 3).
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Beagáin ‘descendant of Beagán’ (see Began ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of any of various European surnames meaning ‘little’, e.g. French Petit , Polish Mały (see Maly 1).

variants.name: Litle
meanings: English: variant of   Little  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Littles
meanings: English: variant of   Little  .

variants.name: Light
meanings: English: from Middle English liht ‘light, active’ (Old English lēoht).
English: topographic name from Middle English liht, leht ‘light’ (Old English lēoht), used for a ‘light place, clearing, glade’.
English: nickname from Middle English lit ‘little’ (Old English lȳt). Compare Little .

variants.name: Littell
meanings: English: variant of   Little  .

variants.name: Lytle
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (mainly northern Ireland): variant of   Little  .

variants.name: Lyttle
meanings: Irish (mainly northern Ireland): variant of English   Little  .

variants.name: Littel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel.
English: variant of   Little  .
South German (Bavaria): unexplained. This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace).

variants.name: Petit
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Michel, Normand, Mireille, Philippe, Pierre, Achille, Armand, Jean Claude, Jean Jacques, Ketly, Adrien.

French, Walloon, Catalan, English, Jewish (from southern France), West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from (Old) French, Catalan, and Middle English petit ‘small’, hence a nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a big man), or a distinguishing name for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name. Compare Little , Petee , and Petite .

History: Charles Petit from Anneville-Ambourville in Seine-Maritime, France, married Jeanne Rossignol (Martin and Renée Desjardins) in Quebec City, QC, in 1670. Nicolas Petit dit Lapré/Laprée from Le Gué-d'Alleré in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie Pouponnel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. Pierre Petit dit Gobin from Lyon in Rhône married Marguerite Véron in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1692. Pierre Petit dit Saint-Pierre from Évreux in Eure, France, married Judith Miville in Quebec City, QC, in 1758; see also St. Pierre . — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Lighthill
meanings: English: variant of   Little  , modified so as to resemble a placename.

nameMeanings.name: Malden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Malden (Surrey), Maldon (Essex), or Maulden (Bedfordshire) (see   Mauldin  ).

variants.name: Mauldin
meanings: English: habitational name from Maldon (Essex), Maulden (Bedfordshire), or Malden (Surrey). The placenames derive from Old English mǣl ‘monument’, ‘cross’ (crucifix) + dūn ‘hill’, and mean ‘hill with a cross or monument’.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Lorraine   or variant spelling of   Laurie .

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin denoting a migrant from the province of Lorraine in eastern France. This derives its name from Latin Lotharingia ‘territory of the people of Lothar’. The latter is a Germanic personal name derived from hlud ‘fame’ + heri, hari ‘army’. Lorraine began to be used as a girl's name in Scotland in the 19th century, and for a time in the second half of the 20th century enjoyed great popularity, which has since waned.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III was born in 1478, in Danbury, Essex, England, his father, Thomas Darcy, was 19 and his mother, Lady Margaret Harleston, was 23. He married Elizabeth Wentworth about 1504, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 30 September 1508, in Maldon, Essex, England, at the age of 30.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III est né en 1478, à Danbury, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Darcy, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Harleston, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Wentworth environ 1504, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 30 septembre 1508, à Maldon, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 30 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III 1478 in Danbury, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Darcy 19 und seine Mutter Lady Margaret Harleston 23. Er hat um 1504 in Essex, England Elizabeth Wentworth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 30. September 1508 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Maldon, Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III è nato il nel 1478 nel Danbury, Essex, England, il suo padre, Thomas Darcy, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Lady Margaret Harleston, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Wentworth circa 1504, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 30 settembre 1508, nel Maldon, Essex, England, all'età di 30 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden IIIが1478年にイギリスエセックスダンブリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Darcyは19歳，母Lady Margaret Harlestonは23歳であった。約1504年にイギリスエセックスでElizabeth Wentworthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1508年9月30日にイギリスエセックスマルドンで30歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III(이)가 1478년 영국 에세스 단아버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Darcy(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret Harleston(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1504년 영국 에세스에서 Elizabeth Wentworth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1508년 9월 30일 영국 에세스 맬던에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III nasceu em 1478, em Danbury, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Darcy, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret Harleston, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Wentworth aproximadamente 1504, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de setembro de 1508, em Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra, com 30 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III родился в 1478 г. в местности данбури, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Darcy было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Harleston, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Wentworth около 1504 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 30 сентября 1508 г. в местности малдон, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 30 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Robert Darcy van Little Malden III nació en 1478, en Danbury, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Darcy, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret Harleston, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Wentworth aproximadamente 1504, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 30 de septiembre de 1508, en Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 30 años.
